nodes.x: AND now, king Benjamin thought it was expedient, after having finished speaking to the people, that he should take the names of all those who had entered into a covenant with God to keep his…
And it came to pass that there was not one soul, except it were little children, but who had entered into the covenant and had taken upon them the name of Christ.
And again, it came to pass that when king Benjamin had made an end of all these things, and had consecrated his son Mosiah to be a ruler and a king over his people, and had given him all the charges…

Finally, to ensure that the people understood his words and kept their covenants with God to keep His commandments, King Benjamin “appointed priests to teach the people, that thereby they might hear and know the commandments of God, and to stir them up in remembrance of the oath which they had made” (Mosiah 6:3). And Mosiah began to reign in his father's stead. And he began to reign in the thirtieth year of his age, making in the whole, about four hundred and seventy-six years from the time that Lehi left…
13 Shallum the son of Jabesh. began to reign in the nine and thirtieth year of Uzziah king of Judah ; and he: reigned a full month in Samaria.
And king Benjamin lived three years and he died.
All the days of Keinan were nine hundred and ten years, and he died.
All the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years; and he died.
All the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years, and he died.
And all the days of Sheth were nine hundred and twelve years, and he died.
All the days of Mahalaleil were eight hundred and ninety-five years, and he died.
And it came to pass that king Mosiah did walk in the ways of the Lord, and did observe his judgments and his statutes, and did keep his commandments in all things whatsoever he commanded him.
